ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chicken salad' class (FOODON_00002809); no specific code exists for the 4-topping customisable variant with egg. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken salad — composite dish with mixed greens, cooked chicken, egg, and variable toppings
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers composite salad dishes with poultry; the 4-topping customisable format is not individually coded. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for customisable chicken salad with egg. SNOMED CT coverage of composite salad dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of chicken salad with egg and four toppings weighs approximately 300 g, representing a typical restaurant or hawker-centre serving.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Salad with Egg (4 Toppings) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for 'Chicken salad with egg (4 toppings)' (record F08010016). All per-100 g values are sourced at confidence 0.95 as recorded in the input metadata.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed green salad with protein (GI ~32)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific customisable chicken-and-egg salad. GI estimated at 32 (low) based on the closest analogous entry in the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed green salad with lean protein and vinaigrette dressing. Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as: (32 × 2.94 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3) / 100 = 2.8 g. Low GI is consistent with the high protein and fibre content and minimal refined carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 230 mg per 100 g; dressing and topping choices can substantially increase sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request dressing on the side, avoid high-sodium toppings such as processed cheese, croutons, or cured meats, and choose a vinaigrette over Caesar or soy-based dressings, which can add 200–400 mg sodium per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (2.94 g/100 g), high protein (7.01 g/100 g), and estimated GI of 32 support glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: This salad is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate content and high protein from chicken and egg, which slow gastric emptying; patients should avoid sweet dressings and starchy toppings such as corn or croutons to maintain a low glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.3 g/100 g), high dietary fibre (1.91 g/100 g), and lean protein profile support cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The lean chicken and egg base provides high-quality protein with low saturated fat, and the vegetable-rich mixed greens contribute dietary fibre that supports LDL cholesterol management; patients should choose olive oil-based dressings and avoid processed or fried toppings to preserve the cardiovascular benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Antioxidant-rich vegetable toppings and low saturated fat; egg yolk provides vitamin D relevant to RA patients on corticosteroids
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should choose anti-inflammatory toppings and an olive oil-based dressing, and monitor for nightshade-related joint flares if tomatoes, bell peppers, or chilli are selected among the four toppings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Salad with Egg (4 Toppings) is a customisable Western-style salad dish consisting of cooked chicken served over a bed of fresh mixed greens, topped with a whole or sliced egg and a choice of four additional toppings such as cherry tomatoes, cucumber, corn, shredded carrots, bell peppers, or croutons, accompanied by a dressing of choice. The dish is assembled cold and is minimally processed, relying on the quality of its fresh ingredients. It has a light, refreshing flavour profile with a satisfying protein-rich base from the chicken and egg. Per 100 g, it provides 85 kcal, 7.01 g protein, 5.02 g fat, 2.94 g carbohydrate, 1.91 g dietary fibre, and 230 mg sodium. The estimated glycemic index is 32 (low), making it suitable for blood sugar management. Clinically, this dish is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension (with dressing control), and cardiovascular disease due to its low saturated fat, high protein, and fibre content. It is halal-certified and gluten-free when crouton toppings are avoided. The dish is commonly found in Singapore hawker centres, cafes, and Western-style food stalls, and is popular as a lunch or light dinner option for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for an exact named entry (F08010016). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which slightly reduces overall certainty for the GI component, but all core macros and secondary nutrients are from an approved primary source.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB source): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this customisable salad dish (F08010016) as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat estimated at 1.3 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central data for comparable chicken-and-egg salad compositions (USDA SR Legacy #05062 and #01129 combined estimate); this is documented as an adapted value. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed green salad with lean protein; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. This is a Western-style dish served in Singapore; no mandatory local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are required under the multilingual rules, as the dish is classified as Western cuisine, not Singaporean cuisine. The dish is tagged halal and keto in the source input; gluten-free flag set to true on the basis that the base dish contains no gluten-containing ingredients, but consumers should verify topping and dressing choices.
